introduction: As we consider shifting our sales model from dealership franchising to a direct-to-customer approach, it's crucial to understand the potential benefits and how to leverage them. This presentation will explore strategies for driving sales and higher margins through this transition.

subsections.heading: Understanding the Direct-to-Customer Model
subsections.content:
• In 2024, our vehicle distribution strategy resulted in an average dealer cost of $38,000 per vehicle.
• The same vehicles were then sold to customers at an average price point of $44,275.
• The difference of $6,275 per unit represents the margins currently captured by dealers.
• Shifting to a direct sales model could potentially redirect these margins to Elexion Automotive.
• The direct-to-customer approach could eliminate unnecessary dealership markup, increasing our margins and reducing costs for the consumer.
• This model could strengthen customer relationships and brand loyalty, driving sales growth.
• The direct sales model could make Elexion more competitive, given the massive revenues of top dealership groups.
• However, careful planning and implementation will be necessary to manage this transition and mitigate potential risks.
subsections.justification: This content highlights the potential benefits of a direct-to-customer model, providing the necessary data to answer the DR question.

subsections.heading: Impact of Sales Model Change on Profit Margins
subsections.content:
• In many industries, a direct-to-customer sales model can lead to an increase in profit margins
• The model can significantly reduce the cost of intermediaries
• It can also lead to a decrease in the cost of inventory management
• However, the model may require significant investment in IT infrastructure
• It may also require the company to take on the cost of logistics and delivery
• The model can lead to improved cash flow due to faster payment cycles
• Lastly, the model may lead to an increase in customer loyalty due to personalized service
subsections.justification: The content discusses general impacts of a sales model change on profit margins, not specific to Elexion's situation.

subsections.heading: Strategic Approaches for Sales Growth in 2027
subsections.content:
• Emphasizing on improving product quality and innovation
• Investing in research and development to stay ahead of the competition
• Expanding into new markets to increase customer base
• Strengthening customer relationships through improved after-sales services
• Focusing on digital marketing to increase brand visibility
• Collaborating with influencers and celebrities for promotional activities
• Implementing customer loyalty programs to increase repeat purchases
subsections.justification: The content lists general strategies for sales growth not specific to Elexion's transition to a direct-to-customer model.

conclusion: Transitioning to a direct-to-customer sales model presents opportunities for increased sales and profit margins. To capitalize on this, we need to carefully strategize our approach, prioritizing customer experience, and leveraging technology for effective sales operations.
